description:
## The change

Document termination of \`type_module\`'s worklist in a doc-comment
block. Add a \`debug_assert!\` tripwire keyed off the per-spec visit
count.

## The proof (goes in the doc comment above \`type_module\`)

The worklist terminates because:

  (a) \`effective_returns\` updates only via \`Descr::union\`, which is
      monotone w.r.t. inclusion. Therefore \`effective_returns\` is
      monotonically non-decreasing in the product Descr lattice.

  (b) The Descr lattice has finite height H, bounded by the count of
      distinct type-axis values in the program (finite set of atoms,
      ints, floats, tuple arities, list shapes, etc).

  (c) A spec is enqueued only under three conditions:
       1. First emission (occurs at most once per spec key).
       2. A callee's effective_return that this spec reads has
          changed (occurs at most H times per (spec, callee-edge) pair).
       3. An opaque arity newly entered \`opaque_arities\`, triggering
          drain of a pending MakeClosure stmt (occurs at most |arities|
          times per affected spec).

  (d) Spec keys for SCC-internal recursion converge within \`WIDEN_AT\`
      iterations via \`crate::typer::widen\`.

  Therefore the total number of worklist pops is bounded by

      O( |specs| · (1 + H · |return-edges per spec|) )

  which is finite.

## The tripwire

\`\`\`rust
const VISIT_HARD_BOUND: usize = 4096;
// Debug-only safety net. The termination proof above means we can
// never reach this in practice; a hit is a contract violation
// (likely non-monotone Descr op or a missing fast-path miss). Not
// a safety margin — it's a tripwire.
debug_assert!(
    *visit_count.get(&spec_key).unwrap_or(&0) < VISIT_HARD_BOUND,
    "spec {:?} visited {} times — termination invariant violated",
    spec_key,
    visit_count[&spec_key],
);
\`\`\`

## Why a constant, not a counter-bound test

PROV.4's counter bounds are *behavioral* tests — they assert "the
typer doesn't regress on a specific program." This is a *contract*
assertion — "the algorithm itself terminates." The two are
complementary:

  - PROV.4 catches "you introduced an inefficiency."
  - PROV.7 catches "you broke an invariant the algorithm depends on."

The hard bound is generous (4096) because no real program should
get anywhere near it. A trip is a bug, not noise.

## Acceptance

- Doc comment above \`type_module\` contains the four-clause proof.
- \`debug_assert!\` in \`process_worklist\` keyed off visit_count.
- Zero release-build cost (debug_assert compiles out).
- All tests pass; PROV.4 counters well under the hard bound.

## Independent of

PROV.1-5. Depends on PROV.6 (the proof is about the unified worklist
shape, not the current shape).
title: fz-PROV.7: termination proof + debug-build counter-bound tripwire
